text:
```json
[
  "Machine Learning (ML), a fundamental branch of Artificial Intelligence (AI), enables computers to learn patterns from data using input features and target labels to make predictions or decisions without explicit programming, improving accuracy with more training data; Deep Learning (DL) is a specialized part utilizing multi-layered neural networks for complex tasks like image, audio, and natural language processing.",
  "ML systems employ diverse paradigms, including supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement, semi-supervised, and self-supervised learning, processing both structured and unstructured data typically split into training, validation, and test sets, with preprocessing techniques to manage issues like the curse of dimensionality; this is all part of a systematic workflow from data collection to deployment, often implemented using Python and its libraries.",
  "With applications spanning recommendation systems, healthcare, and computer vision, ML models face performance issues like overfitting and underfitting, managed by balancing the bias-variance tradeoff, and present key challenges in data quality, model complexity, interpretability, and responsible ethical practices concerning bias, fairness, and privacy."
]
```
json_data: Machine Learning (ML), a fundamental branch of Artificial Intelligence (AI), enables computers to learn patterns from data using input features and target labels to make predictions or decisions without explicit programming, improving accuracy with more training data; Deep Learning (DL) is a specialized part utilizing multi-layered neural networks for complex tasks like image, audio, and natural language processing.

ML systems employ diverse paradigms, including supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement, semi-supervised, and self-supervised learning, processing both structured and unstructured data typically split into training, validation, and test sets, with preprocessing techniques to manage issues like the curse of dimensionality; this is all part of a systematic workflow from data collection to deployment, often implemented using Python and its libraries.

With applications spanning recommendation systems, healthcare, and computer vision, ML models face performance issues like overfitting and underfitting, managed by balancing the bias-variance tradeoff, and present key challenges in data quality, model complexity, interpretability, and responsible ethical practices concerning bias, fairness, and privacy.
